title: Board Decision Under Political Pressure
rawSituation: The board is being asked to approve a major acquisition. Two non-executive directors have expressed serious reservations about the strategic fit. The CEO is pushing for approval before the year-end. One NED has threatened to resign if the acquisition proceeds without a full due diligence period. The AGM is in three months.

kernelResult.situationSummary: This is a board-level governance decision.
kernelResult.kernelInterpretation: The system interprets this situation as a governance or board-level decision requiring proper process, documentation, and fiduciary consideration. Dimensions surfaced: authority.

authorityState.limitation: May require board ratification for major decisions

obligationState.description: Fiduciary duty to board and shareholders

constraintGraph.description: Board approval required — decision cannot proceed without it

Relevant contracts, filings, or regulatory correspondence
Evidence of constraints (financial statements, timelines, capacity data)
Any documentation referenced in the evidence graph
questionsToAsk: What is the strength of the legal position and what are the limitation periods?
What are the director duties and potential liabilities in this situation?

whatToDoNext: Prepare the documentation listed in the professional brief.
Engage a qualified professional for the regulated element.
Return to the system with the professional's input to update the case.

minimumViablePath.description: Do not bring the decision to a vote until all material reservations are formally documented and due diligence is confirmed as complete or consciously waived. A vote taken before objections are recorded is vulnerable to challenge.
minimumViablePath.rationale: A NED resignation threat is a governance signal, not a negotiating tactic. The process protects the board as much as the decision.

minimumViablePath.description: Ensure any NED reservation or objection is formally minuted before any vote is called. This protects the directors who object and documents the governance record.
minimumViablePath.rationale: Undocumented objections cannot be relied upon later. The record must show the objections were heard.

minimumViablePath.description: Establish whether the CEO's year-end deadline is a legal or contractual obligation or an internal preference. If it is a preference, it does not override governance process.
minimumViablePath.rationale: Manufactured urgency is a known governance pressure vector. Distinguish it from genuine obligation before it determines the timeline.

forbiddenActions.action: Put the decision to a vote while material reservations or resignation threats from directors remain undocumented
forbiddenActions.reason: A vote taken before formal objections are recorded creates reversal risk and may constitute a governance failure. The record must show objections were heard.

whatMustNotBeDelayed: Do not bring the decision to a vote until all material reservations are formally documented and due diligence is confirmed as complete or consciously waived. A vote taken before objections are recorded is vulnerable to challenge.
Ensure any NED reservation or objection is formally minuted before any vote is called. This protects the directors who object and documents the governance record.
